arguments.task: Research Coda using official first-party sources only where possible. Visit the Coda developers landing page, then find and extract evidence for: API authentication methods, whether API access is self-serve or gated, API type and breadth, rate limits, and any official or first-party signals about MCP or AI agent/server support. Also find official pricing or plan pages relevant to API/buildability if available. Return concise findings with exact source URLs and quoted supporting text snippets.

input.thought: Start a browser research run on official Coda sources to collect verifiable evidence for API, auth, access, rate limits, and MCP availability.

arguments.task: Using official Coda pages only, extract exact evidence and URLs for these items: 1) auth method(s) for the API, including whether bearer tokens are used and whether OAuth exists; 2) whether API access is self-serve on free plans or requires paid/gated access; 3) API type and breadth with examples of resources/endpoints; 4) exact published rate limits; 5) any official Coda MCP page or guide proving MCP exists. Also visit https://coda.io/pricing if needed. Return final output with bullet points, exact URLs, and short quotes.

input.thought: Run a narrower second pass to get explicit final evidence and quotes from official Coda pages only.

raw_result.description: Collaborative docs and tables platform with a public developer API for automating docs, rows, tables, pages, and related workspace content.
raw_result.auth_notes: Official API docs indicate Bearer token authentication for requests; Coda's developers site also exposes OAuth authorization docs, but the exact scope and current recommended auth path were not fully verified in the gathered excerpts, so OAuth should be human-checked.
raw_result.gating_reason: Public developer docs are open, and gathered evidence indicates API use is available for free and paid workspaces, though some actions may depend on workspace role/permissions.

api_surface.breadth: Public REST API with documented resources for docs, pages, tables, rows, formulas, controls, webhooks, analytics, and workspace-related operations on the developers site.
api_surface.mcp_notes: Official Coda guide references a Coda MCP experience/server for AI clients and agent workflows, but branding appears mixed with 'Superhuman Docs', so the exact product naming should be human-checked.

raw_result.blocker: Core API is public and self-serve, but the gathered evidence did not fully verify the exact current auth mix and MCP branding/details, so a first toolkit is buildable with token auth while OAuth/MCP specifics may need manual confirmation.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Gathered evidence from the API docs indicates published per-second and per-minute rate limits, but the exact numeric limits were not captured verbatim in the available tool output and should be human-verified from the rate-limits section before downstream use.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official developer hub is public; browser evidence indicated bearer-token auth, documented rate limits, coverage across major document/table resources, and an official MCP guide for AI workflows. Evidence also suggested API availability on free and paid workspaces with some role-based restrictions for certain actions such as creating docs.
raw_result.human_review_reason: Several fields rely on partial extracted evidence rather than verbatim captured passages: exact rate-limit numbers, the current status/scope of OAuth versus token auth, and the precise official naming/details of Coda's MCP offering.
